message:
fix(search): HNSW file integrity validation + adaptive lexical fetch sizing

ann_index.rs — Add comprehensive truncation and corruption detection to
HNSW binary deserialization.  Every read_exact call now goes through a
tracked helper that validates remaining file bytes before attempting the
read, producing clear diagnostics ("HNSW file truncated while reading
<field>: need N bytes, have M") instead of opaque I/O errors.  Also
validates graph/data section lengths against file size and rejects
trailing bytes after the payload.  Includes a test that constructs a
truncated .chsw file and asserts the load is correctly rejected.

query.rs — Replace the fixed 3x over-fetch multiplier in lexical search
with a two-pass adaptive strategy:
  - Small pages (≤16 target hits): fetch 2x first pass
  - Larger pages: fetch 1.5x first pass
  - If deduplication causes shortfall AND initial fetch was saturated,
    retry at 3x to preserve correctness on duplicate-heavy corpora
This reduces common-case work for the majority of searches while
maintaining the same correctness guarantee via fallback.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
